name: DesignDecisions
description: Design decision log for Harmony Design System - documenting key choices, rationale, and alternatives

decisions.rationale: Provides finer control for dense enterprise UIs where precision matters. 8px increments are too coarse for complex forms and data-heavy interfaces.

alternatives.rejected: Too coarse for enterprise data density requirements
Not divisible by common sizes, harder mental math

impact: All spacing tokens use 4px increments (--space-1 = 4px, --space-2 = 8px, etc.)
Component padding/margin follow 4px grid
Half-steps available (--space-0-5 = 2px, --space-1-5 = 6px) for fine-tuning

decisions.decision: Maximum 6 semantic variants per interactive component (Button has 6: primary, secondary, tertiary, outline, ghost, destructive)
decisions.rationale: Prevents variant explosion while providing enough hierarchy. 6 variants cover: 3 hierarchy levels (primary/secondary/tertiary), 2 style variations (outline/ghost), and 1 semantic (destructive).

alternatives.rejected: Too limiting for enterprise use cases

alternatives.option: 10+ variants with visual names (blue, red, large, small)
alternatives.rejected: Creates API complexity, breaks semantic meaning

impact: Button has 6 variants as the maximum
Badge has 9 variants (includes more semantic states: success, warning, error, info, orange, pink)
New components should aim for 4-6 variants
Variants must be semantic (primary, success) not visual (blue, rounded)

decisions.decision: Always use Icon component, never inline SVG paths
decisions.rationale: Centralizes icon management, enables consistent sizing/theming, makes icons swappable without touching component code. 34 of 49 components use Icon.

alternatives.rejected: Duplicates code, hard to update icons globally
Performance issues, aliasing problems, harder to customize

impact: Single import: import Icon from './Icon.astro'
Consistent sizing via size prop (xs, sm, md, lg)
Automatic theme color inheritance
Easy to swap icon library (currently Tabler Icons)

decisions.decision: Use numbered radius convention: radius-04, radius-08, radius-12, radius-16, radius-24, radius-100
decisions.rationale: Numbers indicate exact pixel values, making it intuitive. radius-100 (9999px) is clearly 'fully rounded'. More predictable than t-shirt sizing.

alternatives.rejected: Doesn't communicate actual values, harder to choose
Too specific, limits reusability

impact: Clear naming: radius-08 = 8px
radius-100 = 9999px (fully rounded pills/circles)
Easy to calculate: need 12px? Use radius-12

decisions.decision: Use semantic color tokens (bg-card, text-title, border-theme) instead of raw hex values
decisions.rationale: Enables theme switching and dark mode. Semantic names communicate intent. Raw hex values break when themes change.

alternatives.rejected: Breaks theming, impossible to support dark mode
Not semantic, doesn't communicate purpose

impact: All colors use CSS custom properties: var(--theme-primary)
Automatic dark mode support via token swapping
4 themes × 2 modes = 8 theme combinations supported
Components work in any theme without modification

decisions.decision: CP theme uses FloatingNav below header, no footer; VP/PPM/Maconomy use footer navigation
decisions.rationale: CP product has different navigation pattern from other products. FloatingNav provides prominent workspace switching. Other products prefer footer tabs.

alternatives.option: All themes use same navigation (footer or floating)
alternatives.rejected: Doesn't match existing product navigation patterns
Too much code duplication

impact: Single ShellLayout component with conditional rendering
CP: 88px top padding to accommodate FloatingNav
VP/PPM/Maconomy: 20px top padding, footer at bottom
Grid structure differs: CP (2 rows), others (3 rows)

decisions.decision: Use vanilla CSS with custom properties, no CSS-in-JS or Tailwind
decisions.rationale: Maximum compatibility, no build-time dependencies, simple mental model, excellent performance, works with any framework.

alternatives.rejected: Adds build complexity, utility classes verbose in Astro, harder to override
Runtime overhead, framework-specific, harder to share styles
Adds build step, CSS custom properties are native now

impact: All styles in src/styles/ as plain CSS
CSS custom properties for theming (--space-4, --theme-primary)
BEM naming convention for components
No JavaScript required for styling
Easy to copy styles to any project

decisions.decision: Use Astro for documentation site and component demos
decisions.rationale: Zero JS by default, fast builds, component islands for interactivity, excellent DX, perfect for documentation sites.

alternatives.rejected: Heavier, more React-specific, overkill for documentation
Less flexible for component demos, more opinionated
Too heavy for simple documentation, complex setup

impact: Fast documentation site (static HTML)
Components are .astro files (framework-agnostic)
Interactive demos via islands
Easy to deploy (static files)

decisions.decision: Use Lexend for display/headings, Figtree for body text, JetBrains Mono for code
decisions.rationale: Lexend is highly legible for headings, Figtree is clean and modern for UI/body, JetBrains Mono is excellent for code. All are Google Fonts (free, well-supported).

alternatives.rejected: Less distinctive, no brand personality
Doesn't provide enough visual hierarchy
Licensing costs, distribution complexity

impact: Lexend loaded for headings (weights 400-900)
Figtree loaded for body (weights 300-900)
JetBrains Mono loaded for code (weights 400-600)
Total font load: ~150KB compressed

decisions.decision: Use xs, sm, md, lg for size scale (not tiny, small, medium, large, huge)
decisions.rationale: Short, conventional, matches industry standards (Bootstrap, Tailwind, Material UI). Easy to type, clear hierarchy.

alternatives.rejected: Not intuitive, harder to remember
Too verbose for props

alternatives.option: More granularity (xxs, xs, sm, md, lg, xl, xxl)
alternatives.rejected: Too many options, adds complexity

impact: Consistent size prop across components: size="md"
4 sizes cover 99% of use cases
Icons scale with component sizes via mapping

decisions.decision: All components accept class prop for custom styling
decisions.rationale: Provides escape hatch for one-off customization without modifying component. Standard React/Astro pattern.

alternatives.rejected: Too rigid, forces users to copy/modify components
Inline styles harder to maintain, lower specificity

impact: Every component: class?: string prop
Usage: <Button class="custom-class">
Merged with component classes: ['btn', 'btn--primary', className]

decisions.decision: Generate MCP data as committed JSON files (mcp-data/), not parsed on-the-fly
decisions.rationale: 10-100x faster than parsing .astro files. Data is versioned in Git, travels with design system. DSManager can read immediately.

alternatives.rejected: Slow, requires AST parsing, complex
Adds infrastructure complexity, not portable

impact: mcp-data/ folder with design-tokens.json, components/*.json, layouts/*.json
npm run generate:mcp-data regenerates data
DSManager reads JSON directly (fast)
Data committed to Git, auto-discovered

decisions.decision: Add guidelines/ folder to mcp-data/ with structured JSON rules
decisions.rationale: Enables AI to generate Harmony-compliant components by understanding rules, patterns, and decisions. Inspired by 'Taste 1.0' concept from design systems article.

alternatives.rejected: Harder for AI to query, not structured
Duplicates rules across components, harder to maintain
Not accessible to DSManager MCP tools

impact: mcp-data/guidelines/ with 4 JSON files: component-rules, token-rules, theme-rules, decisions
DSManager can expose via new MCP tools (get_harmony_guidelines, search_harmony_guidelines)
AI gets Harmony-specific context when generating components
Single source of truth for design system conventions

decisions.decision: Use functional categories (form, display, navigation, feedback, layout) rather than Atomic Design terminology (atoms, molecules, organisms) for component organization
decisions.rationale: Functional categories are more intuitive for developers and designers. They describe what components do (form input, display content, navigation) rather than arbitrary complexity levels. Atomic Design principles still guide composition internally, but aren't exposed in naming or docs.

alternatives.option: Use Atomic Design categories explicitly (atoms, molecules, organisms, templates)
alternatives.rejected: Too abstract, requires explaining methodology to every new developer. Doesn't clearly communicate component purpose.

alternatives.option: No categories, flat list of all components
alternatives.rejected: Makes it hard to find components and understand relationships
Adds cognitive overhead without clear benefit

impact: Components organized in manifest.json by functional categories
Documentation groups components by what they do, not complexity level
Atomic Design mapping documented in category-rules.json for internal understanding
New developers can intuitively find components (need form input? Check 'form' category)
decisions.note: Atomic Design hierarchy documented in guidelines for composition guidance, but not used in public API

decisions.decision: Categorize components as 'form' if they manage validation/state, 'layout' if they only arrange visually
decisions.rationale: CheckboxGroup vs ButtonGroup illustrates the distinction: CheckboxGroup manages validation state for multiple checkboxes (form category), while ButtonGroup only arranges buttons visually (layout category). The key differentiator is data/state management, not visual arrangement.

alternatives.option: Put all grouping components in 'layout' category
alternatives.rejected: Loses semantic meaning - CheckboxGroup IS a form component that participates in validation
Doesn't clarify the fundamental difference (state management vs pure visual)
Some components (ButtonGroup, Stepper) truly are pure layout without semantic meaning

impact: Clear criteria for categorization (documented in category-rules.json)
CheckboxGroup, RadioGroup in 'form' - they handle validation
ButtonGroup, Stepper in 'layout' - they only arrange visually
Prevents confusion about why similar-looking components are in different categories

decisionCriteria.form: Accepts user input, has value, participates in validation, can be disabled/required
decisionCriteria.layout: Arranges other components, controls spacing/positioning, no data handling

decisions.decision: Use props for configuration, slots for content; prefer composition over configuration objects
decisions.rationale: Slots provide maximum flexibility for content (any HTML/components), while props provide type safety for configuration (variants, sizes, states). Complex configuration objects are harder to type, document, and use than composing via slots.

alternatives.rejected: Loses flexibility, requires serializing all content to props, verbose API
Loses type safety for simple configuration, harder to validate

alternatives.option: Complex config objects for all composition
alternatives.rejected: Hard to type (TypeScript), hard to document, inflexible for custom content

impact: Button uses props: variant, size, disabled (configuration)
Card uses slots: header, footer, actions (content)
Input uses hybrid: label prop (simple string) OR Label component (composition)
Documented in component-rules.json composition section

guidelines.useSlots: Complex content, HTML structure, component composition
guidelines.useHybrid: Provide prop for common case, slot for flexibility (like Input label)

examples.correct: Card with header slot accepting any content
examples.incorrect: Card with headerConfig={{ title, subtitle, icon, actions: [...] }}

pendingDecisions.question: How should tables handle mobile responsive behavior?
Should we add a formal animation/transition system?

rejectedDecisions.decision: Do not add 'outline' variant to Badge component
rejectedDecisions.rationale: Badge is for status/labels, doesn't need outline variant. Button has outline because it needs visual hierarchy.
rejectedDecisions.impact: Badge has 9 variants but not outline

changes: Initial Harmony Design System with Tailwind CSS
Added multi-theme support (CP, VP, PPM, Maconomy)
Added dark mode for all themes
Added MCP data generation (design-tokens.json, components/*.json, layouts/*.json)
Added category-rules.json with form patterns, layout patterns, atomic design mapping
Expanded component-rules.json with atomic design hierarchy and advanced composition patterns
Documented key design decisions: functional categories, forms vs layouts, slots vs props
